condensed_reasoning:
## Research

I was forecasting **ECDC's cumulative Bundibugyo Ebola death figure** for the DRC/Uganda outbreak in the first Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) published on/after **July 22, 2026** — likely the Fri July 24 CDTR with a data cut ~July 22.

I started by organizing the known death trajectory into a Monte Carlo simulation. My initial anchor was the last ECDC figure: **625 deaths (data to Jul 8, published Jul 10)**. I noted a tension between the fast epidemic growth (WHO 232 Jun 15 → 452 Jul 1; CDC 580 Jul 6, implying ~15–25 deaths/day) and ECDC's own apparent weekly increment of only +25. I built a mixture model (slow ECDC-lag ~28%, moderate linear ~40%, fast exponential ~26%, rare reclassification surge ~6%) over a 14-day horizon, producing a **median ~924, P10 678, P90 1145**.

## Key Evidence

My premortem review caught a **critical flaw**: my own fresh worldview research (`uganda-...-1b0386de`, updated same day) recorded **NICD DRC = 702 confirmed deaths as of July 11** — a data point I had ignored. That is +77 deaths in 3 days (Jul 8→11) ≈ **26/day**, squarely on the fast trajectory and flatly contradicting my "slow ECDC cadence" Factor 3. The multi-source trajectory **CDC 580 (Jul 6) → ECDC 625 (Jul 8) → NICD 702 (Jul 11)** confirmed a consistent ~24 deaths/day. I recognized the apparent "ECDC +25/week" increment was a **lagging-snapshot artifact**, not the true cadence.

## Conclusion

I re-anchored on the freshest data point (**702 deaths, Jul 11, +~2 Uganda ≈ 704**) and rebuilt the Monte Carlo: a mixture of sustained (~23/day, 60%), decelerating (~14/day, 25%), and accelerating (~32/day, 15%) rates, over a ~10-day horizon to the ~Jul 22 data cut, with triangular data-cut-date uncertainty (Jul 18–24), a 12% ECDC-lag component, a cumulative floor at 702, and a 5% reclassification surge tail. This yielded my final distribution: **median ~918, P10 808, P25 860, P75 978, P90 1045**, with a right-skewed upper tail (P99 ~1295) and a hard floor near 702. I concluded the underlying epidemic was accumulating deaths at ~24/day and that the excluded "slow cadence" pull had been a misreading.

research.notes: Both calls failed; the agent fell back on existing worldview research.

web_search.notes: Not shown in the visible trace but reflected in session metrics; underpinned the worldview research the agent ultimately relied on.

fetch_url.notes: Supported source verification for the death-toll trajectory.

stock_price.notes: A stock_price call on an Ebola question is nonsensical and wasted.

capability_gaps: A tool returning ECDC's raw CDTR death-figure increment history would have directly resolved the central ECDC-lag uncertainty instead of it being modeled as a 12% adjustment.
subtle_bugs: The agent conflates NICD's 702 (DRC-only) with an 'ECDC-equivalent' anchor of ~704 by adding Uganda's 2; this is reasonable but glosses over the possibility that ECDC's CDTR reports a materially different (lower/older) figure than NICD.

classification.approach: Monte Carlo simulation projecting a cumulative death trajectory from the freshest anchor (702 deaths, Jul 11) at ~24 deaths/day over a ~10-day horizon, with a rate mixture, data-cut-date uncertainty, and reporting-lag/surge tails.
classification.key_uncertainty: ECDC's reporting lag — whether the late-July CDTR cites a near-current (~Jul 20–22) data cut or lags ~1 week, which shifts the resolving figure by ~150 deaths.

risk_flags.evidence: The question asks for ECDC's reported figure, but the agent anchors on NICD's DRC count (702, Jul 11). ECDC's own last figure was 625 (Jul 8). The agent assumes ECDC's late-July CDTR will track the NICD/actual count with only a modest lag, but ECDC-specific figures could lag more substantially. This ECDC-vs-actual-source gap is the dominant risk and is only partially modeled by the 12% lag component.

The final distribution places a hard floor at 702 and P10 at 808, but if ECDC's CDTR data cut lags to ~Jul 15 (the agent itself computed ~810–890 for a 1-week lag), the resolving figure could plausibly be in the 800s or high 700s — arguably deserving more lower-tail mass than P10=808 implies given ECDC-specific reporting behavior.

reviewer_estimate.divergence_reason: I weight the ~24–28/day observed rate slightly more and the lag-driven downside slightly less, nudging my central estimate a touch above the agent's 918; but the difference is well within noise and the agent's distribution is a good fit.

strengths: The premortem self-correction loop worked as intended: the review surfaced the ignored 702-deaths (Jul 11) data point from the agent's own worldview store, and the agent fully re-anchored, rebuilt the Monte Carlo, and excluded the contradicted 'slow cadence' factor rather than rationalizing the original number.

Correctly diagnosed the 'ECDC +25/week' increment as a lagging-snapshot artifact rather than the true death-accumulation cadence, using the CDC 580 → ECDC 625 → NICD 702 cross-source trajectory to establish ~24/day.
Cross-checked internal consistency against the sibling confirmed-cases sub-forecast (median 2,120 cases, ~23 new cases/day at ~35% CFR), which independently supports ~25–28 deaths/day.

Modeled the data-cut-date uncertainty explicitly with a triangular(18,21,24) distribution and an ECDC-lag component, correctly identifying reporting lag as the key uncertainty rather than epidemic dynamics.
Applied a hard cumulative floor at 702, respecting the monotonic nature of cumulative death counts.
weaknesses: Initial reflection ignored a fresh, same-day data point (702 deaths, Jul 11) sitting in its own worldview store, anchoring instead on the stale 625 (Jul 8) figure — only caught because the premortem gate forced a review.

The tool_audit in the initial reflection falsely claimed 'no external data tools needed beyond the figures given in the question description,' when the session actually ran 9 web_search + 6 fetch_url calls (plus failed research/stock_price calls); the narrative disagreed with reality.

The core ECDC-vs-source distinction is under-modeled: the agent treats NICD's 702 as an ECDC-equivalent anchor with only a 12% lag component, but ECDC's CDTR could systematically report a lower or older figure than NICD, and this structural source-mismatch risk is not fully separated from generic rate uncertainty.
A stock_price tool call was made on an Ebola epidemiology question — an irrelevant, wasted call.

issues: research tool failed on 100% of calls (2/2)
stock_price tool called once and failed on an Ebola question — irrelevant and wasted

the visible trace_at_premortem.md showed only execute_code calls, omitting the 9 web_search + 6 fetch_url calls that occurred, causing the premortem sub-agent to initially believe research was missing
